hero.label: Product Designer
hero.name: Lior Baum
hero.description: Crafting digital experiences that merge aesthetics with functionality. Based in Israel, currently available for freelance work.

about.label: About Me
bio: I'm a Product & UX Designer who cares about the full process — from user research to scalable design systems to interfaces that make complex things feel simple.

What sets me apart is where design meets technology. I teach designers at Shenkar to build real products using AI-assisted tools like Cursor and Claude, and I bring that same mindset to my own work. I'm not just delivering Figma files — I prototype in code, move faster, and collaborate with engineers from a place of real understanding.
I thrive in environments where design has actual ownership over the product, not just the pixels.

experience.description: Improved key parts of the iTero™ scanner experience, focusing on how doctors and patients move through the system. Redesigned navigation and content structure within the iTero™ Store to enhance discoverability and usability. Designed the full UX experience around scanning – from patient onboarding and scan setup to post-scan insights and next steps.

Assisting in a new interdisciplinary course that teaches designers to build functional products using AI-assisted coding tools. Supporting students in translating design concepts into working prototypes using Cursor, Claude, and modern web frameworks. Providing one-on-one mentorship and code reviews, bridging the gap between design thinking and technical implementation.

Led the redesign of the asset management panel within a complex privacy system. Designed flows that supported automated risk detection in sensitive data environments. Partnered with privacy officers and PMs to refine logic and improve system trust and usability.

Reimagined clinician dashboard to reduce friction in digital physical therapy sessions. Conducted interviews with stakeholders and therapists to identify pain points and deliver improved task flows. Created onboarding flows and visual guidance assets to support product adoption.
